questions.question: According to the DSM-5, substance use disorder is diagnosed based on meeting at least how many criteria within a 12-month period?
questions.explanation: The DSM-5 requires at least two of eleven criteria to be met within a 12-month period for a diagnosis of substance use disorder. Severity is rated on a continuum: mild (2-3 criteria), moderate (4-5), or severe (6 or more).

questions.question: Which statement about relapse in substance use disorders is most accurate?

options.text: Relapse indicates that treatment has completely failed and should be discontinued
Relapse is common and should be viewed as a part of the recovery process
Relapse only occurs in people who did not receive medication-assisted treatment
Relapse rates for substance use disorders are much higher than for any other chronic condition

questions.explanation: Relapse is a common part of the recovery process for substance use disorders, with rates (40-60%) comparable to other chronic conditions like diabetes and hypertension. It should be viewed as an indication that treatment needs adjustment, not that it has failed.
